qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via cluster node registration and proxy middleware allows authenticated users to access internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding traces arbitrary cluster node URL registration and proxy forwarding through /api/nodes and internal/middleware/proxy.go; it does not identify the advisory's missing WebSocket Origin validation or cookie-authenticated CSWSH path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory concerns Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking caused by websocket.Upgrader CheckOrigin accepting all origins on authenticated nginx-ui WebSocket endpoints, with browser cookie authentication. The qualifying finding instead reports an SSRF proxy path.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-78mf-482w-62qj describes permissive CheckOrigin functions in Go WebSocket endpoint files such as api/terminal/pty.go, api/event/websocket.go, api/cluster/websocket.go, and related endpoints.
ssrf-cluster-node-proxy/vulnerability_report.json cites AddNode/EditNode and internal/middleware/proxy.go reverse proxy behavior, not WebSocket origin checks.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so runtime tiering is not applicable.
